auth.title: TeslaHub
auth.subtitle: Connect to your dashboard
auth.tooManyAttempts: Too many attempts. Try again later.

control.asleepHint: Vehicle is asleep — tap a button to wake it before sending a command.
control.staleHint: Vehicle is asleep — values shown come from your local TeslaMate cache. Some advanced settings (cabin overheat, seat heaters, bioweapon, valet, software update) won't be visible until you wake the car.

configure.title: Fleet API not configured
configure.body: Set up your Tesla developer credentials in Settings to enable remote control.

connect.title: Tesla account not connected
connect.body: Connect your Tesla account in Settings → Tesla integration.

syncVehicles.title: No vehicle synced
syncVehicles.body: Sync your vehicles in Settings → Tesla integration.

pairKey.title: Pair the virtual key
pairKey.body: {{name}} cannot accept commands until the TeslaHub virtual key is paired from the Tesla mobile app.

vinMismatch.title: Vehicle not found in Fleet API
vinMismatch.body: {{name}} is in TeslaMate but is not synced as a Fleet API vehicle yet. Open Settings → Tesla integration and re-sync your vehicles, then pair the virtual key for this car.

climate.title: Climate
climate.preconditionHint: Tesla's "Defrost" mode: blasts the climate to max heat + windshield defrost. Same button as the in-car app.

keeperHint.off: Climate stops as usual when you park and lock the car.
keeperHint.on: Keeps the climate running after lock + nobody inside (no auto-shutoff).
keeperHint.dog: Pet mode — keeps the cabin around 21°C and shows a reassuring message on the screen for passers-by.
keeperHint.camp: Camp mode — keeps climate, audio and USB power running through the night.

charge.title: Charging
charge.unlockCableHint: Releases the charging cable lock so you can unplug it.

access.title: Access
access.speedLimit.pinHint: Enter your 4-digit Tesla speed limit PIN.
access.valet.pinHint: Enter the 4-digit PIN to set or clear valet mode.

cancelConfirm.title: Cancel software update?
cancelConfirm.body: The update will not install. You can re-schedule it later if it is still available.

navigation.openMap: Send a destination from the map

home.costExplain: Shows how much of your accumulated charge costs ({{total}}) you've consumed so far, based on your actual battery usage. Accounts for multiple recent charges when your battery level drops below the start of the latest one.
home.tripWaitingDb: Trip detected via MQTT — TeslaMate hasn't recorded it yet.

charging.noSessions: No charging sessions for this period

chargingStats.noDcData: No DC charging data available yet.
chargingStats.noDcSession: No DC data for this session.
chargingStats.descSingle: Power (kW) vs State of Charge (%) for this session.
chargingStats.descAll: Power (kW) vs State of Charge (%). Each color = a session. White line = median.

costs.title: Costs
costs.noData: No cost data for this period.
costs.setPrices: Set prices on your charging sessions to see analytics here.
costs.geofenceCosts: Costs come from TeslaMate geofence data.
costs.configureGas: Enter fuel price and consumption to see savings.

warning.missingKeyTitle: Open Charge Map API key required
warning.missingKeyBody: OCM blocks anonymous requests. Get a free key, then paste it under Settings → Public chargers.
warning.rateLimitedBody: Too many requests to Open Charge Map. Try again later or use a personal API key.
warning.errorBody: Couldn't reach OCM right now. Try panning the map again in a moment.

map.liveUnavailable: MQTT not available — live tracking disabled

settings.title: Settings
settings.costSourceTeslahub: Costs are manually entered per charging session in TeslaHub.
settings.costSourceTeslamate: Costs come from TeslaMate geofence data. Manual cost entry is disabled.

chargers.toggleHint: Display Tesla Superchargers and third-party operators (Ionity, Fastned, TotalEnergies, etc.) on the map. Off by default.
chargers.customHint: Partial, case-insensitive match. Example: "Tesla, Ionity" shows every operator whose name contains either word.
chargers.minPowerHint: Hide stations whose declared max power is below the threshold. Stations with no declared power are also hidden when a threshold is set.
chargers.apiKeyHint: Open Charge Map no longer accepts anonymous requests: without a key the layer stays empty. Generate a free key (link below). A key set here takes precedence over the OCM_API_KEY environment variable.
chargers.dataSource: Data from Open Charge Map (CC-BY-SA), cached for 24h server-side.

settings.step1: 1. Go to tesla.com and sign in
settings.invalidUrl: Invalid URL. Must be a Tesla compositor URL.
settings.downloadFailed: Failed to download image. Check the URL.
settings.noLocations: No locations configured. Tap "Define this location" on a charging session to add one.
settings.pwMinLength: Password must be at least 6 characters.
settings.poweredBy: Powered by TeslaMate — read-only, no data modified

vampire.title: Vampire Drain
vampire.noData: No vampire drain data for this period.
vampire.noDataSub: Try reducing the minimum idle time or extending the time range.
vampire.healthDesc: Your car consumes on avg. {{watts}} W at rest — like a {{analogy}}.

tripPage.title: Trip Analyzer
tripPage.description: Select a date range to analyze a multi-stop journey.
tripPage.noData: No drives or charges in this period

locationsPage.title: Visited Locations
locationsPage.heatHint: Switch to Pins to click locations

vehicleView.mqttDisconnected: MQTT not connected — body/security data unavailable
vehicleView.mqttHint: Enable MQTT to see doors, trunk, lock, Sentry Mode and more — see docs

goToCar.title: Go to my car
goToCar.geoDenied: Location access denied. Please allow geolocation in your browser.
goToCar.geoUnavailable: Location not available on this device.
goToCar.geoTimeout: Timed out trying to get your location.
goToCar.disclaimer: Routing is handled by the app you pick. The distance shown is straight-line.

securityAlerts.title: Security Alerts
securityAlerts.subtitle: Optional real-time Tesla Sentry & break-in notifications.
securityAlerts.selfHostNote: Powered by your own Tesla developer app — fully self-hosted.

advancedWarning.title: Advanced setup — for confirmed users
advancedWarning.body: Setting up real-time Tesla alerts means running your own Fleet Telemetry server and Tesla command-signing proxy on your own domain. You will need a public DNS name, a router port forward, a reverse proxy (Caddy / nginx) with Let's Encrypt, two Docker images built locally from Tesla's source code, and some comfort with file permissions inside Docker volumes. The 'fully self-hosted, zero third party' philosophy is great — but everything that a SaaS would normally hide is now your responsibility. If you only need the dashboard, map, and history, leave this feature off; the rest of TeslaHub works perfectly without it.
advancedWarning.guideLabel: Read the full setup guide & troubleshooting →

securityAlerts.creditSuffix: (AGPL-3.0). TeslaHub keeps everything self-hosted: no data ever leaves your server.

feedback.missingParams: Tesla returned an incomplete response. Please try again.

notConfigured.intro: To enable real-time Tesla Sentry alerts, register a Tesla developer app and configure these environment variables on this TeslaHub instance:
notConfigured.guideLink: Open the full Security Alerts setup guide
notConfigured.guideHint: in the TeslaHub README for the step-by-step walkthrough.

notConnected.intro: Sign in with the Tesla account that owns the vehicles you want to monitor. Tokens are encrypted at rest with AES-GCM and never leave your TeslaHub instance.

connectedPanel.confirmDisconnect: Disconnect Tesla account? You will need to sign in again to receive alerts.
connectedPanel.refreshHint: Force an immediate refresh of this account's Tesla tokens.
connectedPanel.addAccountHint: Typical use case: a couple sharing a TeslaHub instance where each spouse owns their own car. Open TeslaHub on the other person's phone and tap this button so Tesla auto-logs them in with THEIR account. Each vehicle will then be contacted with the right owner token.

developerApp.title: 0. Create your Tesla developer app
developerApp.intro: Before everything else, you need a Tesla Fleet API client_id and client_secret. They identify this TeslaHub instance to Tesla. The whole process is free and takes about five minutes.
developerApp.step1: Open developer.tesla.com, click Sign in and use your existing Tesla account credentials.
developerApp.step2: From the left menu, go to Apps, then click Create New App.
developerApp.step3: Fill in the form with the values below — click the copy buttons to avoid typos.
developerApp.step4: Click Submit. Tesla returns a Client ID and a Client Secret — keep them safe, you won't see the secret again.
developerApp.step5: Add these three variables to your TeslaHub .env file:
developerApp.envHint: Then restart the API: docker compose up -d teslahub-api. Refresh this page when done.

wizard.title: Vehicle pairing wizard
wizard.intro: Once your Tesla account is connected, follow these steps to authorize this TeslaHub instance to receive telemetry from your vehicles.

prereqWarning.title: Stop — read this before clicking the buttons below
prereqWarning.body: Each step expects host-side preparation done BEFORE you click. The wizard does not check any of this for you. Going through the buttons in order without the prerequisites in place will fail with cryptic Tesla errors (502, redirect_uri_mismatch, permission denied, etc.).
prereqWarning.step1Items: Public DNS for teslahub.<your domain> resolves to your server | Reverse proxy forwards /.well-known/appspecific/* to teslahub-api (not the React SPA)
prereqWarning.step2Items: Step 1 done | The public-key URL above returns a -----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY----- block when fetched with curl from any external machine
prereqWarning.step3Items: Step 2 done | iPhone with the Tesla mobile app installed and signed in to the Tesla account that owns the cars
prereqWarning.step4Items: DNS-only record for telemetry.<your domain> on Cloudflare (grey cloud, NOT proxied) | Port 8443/tcp forwarded from your router to your server | Caddy obtained a Let's Encrypt cert for telemetry.<your domain> AND made it readable by uid 65532 | fleet-telemetry-src and vehicle-command-src cloned and built | fleet-telemetry/config.json filled with your real domain | fleet-telemetry, tesla-http-proxy-init and tesla-http-proxy containers all UP | Telegram bot token in .env AND at least one recipient added below who has sent /start to your bot | Private key exported to the key-vault volume (orange button below)
prereqWarning.linkLabel: Open the full Security Alerts setup guide →

step1.title: 1. Generate the public key for your domain
step1.intro: TeslaHub creates an EC P-256 keypair and exposes the public key at a fixed well-known URL on your domain. The private key is encrypted at rest with AES-GCM and never leaves the database.
step1.domainHint: Use the host TeslaHub is reachable from on the public internet (no http:// prefix, no path).
step1.publicKeyTestHint: Open this URL in a new tab — it must return your public key in PEM format. If it fails, verify your DNS record, your reverse proxy (Caddy/Nginx), and that the path /.well-known/appspecific/com.tesla.3p.public-key.pem reaches the TeslaHub API container.

step2.title: 2. Register your domain with Tesla
step2.intro: Tells Tesla that your domain hosts a partner application. Tesla fetches the public key from your .well-known endpoint to confirm.

step3.title: 3. Pair each vehicle with TeslaHub
step3.qrIntro: Scan this QR code with your iPhone (or open the link below in your phone's browser):
step3.qrHint: The Tesla mobile app will prompt you to approve TeslaHub's virtual key for the selected vehicle. Repeat this for every car you want to monitor. Once approved, click I've approved next to the matching vehicle.
step3.sharedVehicleWarning: Heads up: vehicles shared with you as a Driver (not Owner) will appear in this list and accept Send-to-car, but signed commands (lock, climate, horn, frunk…) require the OWNER to pair the key from THEIR phone. If a button keeps failing with "key not paired", that's why — TeslaHub will mark the vehicle as not paired automatically on the first failed command.
step3.noVehicles: No vehicles synced yet. Click Sync vehicles from Tesla above.

step4.title: 4. Tell Tesla to start streaming telemetry
step4.intro: Once your fleet-telemetry container is running and reachable on telemetry.yourdomain.com (see README), click below. TeslaHub calls Tesla's /fleet_telemetry_config for every paired vehicle. The call is signed by your tesla-http-proxy sidecar with the partner private key.
step4.configure: Configure telemetry for all paired vehicles
step4.needPaired: Pair at least one vehicle in step 3 first.
step4.exportKeyTitle: Before configuring: export the partner private key
step4.exportKeyHint: Tesla now requires fleet_telemetry_config to be signed by your partner private key. Click below once to write the key (decrypted) to the shared key-vault Docker volume so the tesla-http-proxy container can sign the request.
step4.exportKey: Export private key for the proxy

feedback.registered: Domain registered with Tesla as a partner.
feedback.telemetryConfigured: Telemetry configured with Tesla. Streaming will start when the vehicle is awake.
feedback.keyExported: Partner private key exported to {{path}}. The tesla-http-proxy container can now sign Tesla API requests.
feedback.keyExportError: Could not export the partner private key.

recipients.title: Notification recipients
recipients.intro: Add one entry per person who should receive Telegram alerts. Each recipient can subscribe to one or more vehicles independently. Sentry and break-in alerts can be toggled per (recipient, vehicle) pair.
recipients.chatIdHelp: To find the Telegram chat ID: open Telegram, send any message to @userinfobot; it replies with the numeric id. Important: every new recipient must first open Telegram, search for your TeslaHub bot and send /start before you add them here. Without that, Telegram refuses messages and the test will fail.
recipients.startBotWarning: Required for each new recipient: they must open Telegram, search for your TeslaHub bot and send /start. Without that first message from the recipient to the bot, Telegram blocks delivery (a bot cannot initiate a conversation).
recipients.needPairedVehicle: Pair at least one vehicle to assign subscriptions.

homeTeaser.title: Real-time Sentry alerts available
homeTeaser.body: TeslaHub can send you instant Telegram notifications when Tesla Sentry detects activity around your car. The feature is fully self-hosted — no third-party service required.
homeTeaser.cta: Set it up in Settings → Security Alerts

sendToCar.title: Send a destination
sendToCar.noPinYet: Tap the map or search an address to drop a pin.
sendToCar.noAddressFound: No address found — coordinates will be sent as is.

feedback.missing: Pick at least one vehicle and drop a pin on the map.
feedback.sent: Destination sent. Confirm on the central screen.
feedback.sentTo: Destination sent to {{vehicle}}. Confirm on the central screen.
feedback.sentToMany: Destination sent to {{count}} vehicles ({{vehicles}}). Confirm on each central screen.
feedback.partialFailure: Sent to {{ok}} but failed for {{failed}}. Check the failed car's connection and retry.
feedback.allFailed: Could not send to {{vehicles}}. Make sure the cars are online and try again.
feedback.wakingUp: Waking up the car… Tesla says this can take up to ~60s on a weak cellular signal.
feedback.confirmHint: The car displays a "Start navigation" prompt — accept it to begin.

notConfigured.title: Tesla Fleet API not configured
notConfigured.body: This feature relies on the same Tesla Fleet API setup as the Sentry alerts — a complex integration (Tesla developer app, OAuth, QR-code pairing, reverse proxy). Follow the full guide in Settings → Security Alerts, then come back here.

notConnected.title: Tesla account not connected
notConnected.body: Fleet API is configured but no Tesla account is currently connected. Sign in from Settings → Security Alerts.

noPaired.title: No paired vehicle
noPaired.body: None of your vehicles is paired with the TeslaHub virtual key. The QR-code pairing is mandatory to send any command to a car. Start the wizard from Settings → Security Alerts (step 3).
